fr.elements: Main Building; Front Driveway; Landscaped Area; Pedestrian Walkways
fr.considerations: Limited Rear Access; Single Access Point
fr.recommendations: Enhancing the emergency response capability at your property may benefit from addressing the single access point limitation. To improve responder access and movement, consider incorporating an additional access route or widening the existing driveway to facilitate more efficient entry and exit. Furthermore, ensuring clear and unobstructed pedestrian walkways could aid emergency responders by providing alternative navigation paths around the property. The landscaped area should be maintained in a manner that does not obstruct visibility or hinder movement, allowing responders unobstructed lines of sight to and from the main building. Enhancing rear access with additional pathways or emergency-friendly passages could further support effective responder staging and operations, ensuring access to all sides of the property when needed.

wildfire.conditions: Tree Cluster; Scattered Trees; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Vegetated Perimeter; Open Green Space; Dense Tree Canopy; Vegetation Adjacent to Fence; Defensible Space; Open Terrain
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space around the property could be achieved by increasing separation between the tree clusters and scattered trees, and the structure, which may involve selective thinning or pruning. Managing vegetation adjacent to the structure and the fence could reduce direct fire exposure and support physical barrier functions. The presence of an open green space provides a natural break, which can be preserved to inhibit fire spread. Maintaining open terrain while reducing the density of the tree canopy enhances visibility and access for firefighting efforts, and decreases fuel continuity. Regularly managing the vegetated perimeter with strategic thinning or selective removal can further support property preparedness efforts. Such practices can support long-term wildfire resilience by reducing opportunities for fire to establish and spread on the property.
